ops.title: 3-arm token bench results through v0.11.0 — per-call savings real (90%), session-level T turn-dominated and within ±20% noise; variance bombs identified for the redo
ops.body:
State of the ccx token benchmark as of 2026-07-10 (supersedes the campaign framing in [[ccx-bench-sonnet-opus-result]]).

**Built and shipped**: 3-arm harness (baseline / ccx-mcp / ccx-cli), usage-shaped 26-task corpus (tornado v6.4.1 + click 8.1.7, 100KB traversal floor; mux microbench-only), dual headlines (peak context H, total tokens T, median-across-repeats, paired CIs), per-arm integrity, isolation panel. Two review cycles (codex finder+refuter) fixed 10 + 11 confirmed findings. ccx v0.11.0 released with the three bench-exposed product fixes: `ccx code grep --regex` + multi-file positionals (rg engine route), guard dialect-validator rewrites (`^class ` greps now rewrite instead of blocking; bounded file greps pass under the 20KB threshold), scoped 0-symbol `vcs diff` no longer drops hunks, `git rev-parse` ref validation. Campaign config runs the released brew binary.

**Measured (two sonnet pilots + opus spot, all validity gates green)**:
- Layer 1 (microbench, per-call): **90.0% output-token savings, 0 in-regime violations** on the released binary — real and regression-guarded. Below-200-raw-token pairs labeled, not gated.
- Session level: **no resolvable direction.** T ≈ turns × ~35k (tool-output sizes are rounding error); sonnet/opus baselines are natively frugal (grep-first, bounded reads — the "naive baseline traverses 100KB" premise is false); single-run noise floor is ±20% mean / 34.5% max |ΔT| on identical tasks (deterministic control: 0.2%). ccx-mcp pays a structural ~3-5k/turn schema tax on H (always loses peak context).
- v0.11.0 fixes validated behaviorally: guard blocks 3→1, lc-click thrash eliminated (model uses `--regex` from turn 1), zero budget-cap footers, zero rewrite confusion.

**Why:** the fact that per-run session noise floor exceeds the effect size. Variance bombs: (1) run-the-test-suite-or-not coin flip = ±14 turns / ±150% T on targeted_edit tasks (flipped opposite directions across arms on the same task); (2) edit-click-batch-partial fixture env friction (python2.7 setuptools, no pip) inflates test-running runs; (3) 2 envelope-vs-transcript mismatches (25-33% off) on the v0.11.0 pilot, unexplained; (4) baseline turn-count coin flips (merged vs split ls/find).

**How to apply:** any redo must control variance before spending: pin verification policy symmetrically in prompts, fix/drop the edit fixture friction, repeats ≥5 with per-task medians, consider turns as a primary or diagnostic metric, and investigate the envelope accounting mismatches. Don't cite a session-level savings claim from existing data; the citable claim is layered (per-call 90%, session-level unresolved). Pilot sessions: bench/results/20260709T144422Z (v0.8.1-era), 20260711T010914Z (v0.11.0).
